raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I initially purchased airline tickets online for approximately {$500.00}. Shortly after the purchase, I received a call from the third-party booking company stating the tickets were invalid unless I rebooked at a higher price. I was told baggage was not available to purchase once at the airport. 

During the call, I was pressured to pay an additional {$1000.00} to rebook. Due to a language barrier and misleading information, I believed this charge was replacing the original amount, not in addition to it. I later realized I was being charged both amounts. 

I told the agent I did not agree, would not proceed, and would not take the flight. The agent was rude, dismissive, and ended the call abruptly. I did not travel using these tickets. 

I disputed both charges with Citi. Citi reversed the initial XXXX {$500.00} charge but denied the dispute for the {$1000.00}, stating the charge was non-refundable. The tickets were purchased through a third-party website that, upon further investigation, is known for bait-and-switch practices. I never received valid airline confirmation or a proper cancellation option despite requesting it.
